jobs.description: Compile and display task list from Trello + Calendar
jobs.prompt: Source .env. Fetch all cards from Trello 'A Fazer' list (use TRELLO_KEY and TRELLO_TOKEN from env, list ID from config/trello.json). Check today's calendar events. Compile a clean task list with: 1) Today's agenda (with times), 2) Dev tasks (prioritized), 3) Other tasks, 4) Brevemente items. Save to memory/today-tasks.md. Print the summary.
Good morning briefing: 1) Check today's calendar events, 2) List Trello 'A Fazer' cards, 3) Check for any pending reminders in memory/reminders.md, 4) Summarize what's on for today. Be concise.

You are Ekus running the hourly digest. Working directory: ~/ekus

STEP 1: Fetch tasks from Cloudflare KV
curl -s "https://ekus-dashboard.goncalo-p-gomes.workers.dev/api/tasks"
Parse the markdown: ## Active = current tasks, ## Waiting On = brevemente items.
Each task line: - [ ] **Title** - description

STEP 2: Fetch calendar events
Use the gcal_list_events MCP tool:
- calendarId: primary
- timeMin: today 00:00:00 (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)
- timeMax: today 23:59:59
- timeZone: Europe/Lisbon
Also fetch tomorrow's events (same format, next day).
If calendar fails, note 'Calendário indisponível' and continue.

STEP 3: Format the digest as Slack mrkdwn
:lizard: _Task List — [DayPT] [DD/MM], [HH]:00_

:alarm_clock: _Agenda de Hoje_
• List today's events. ~strikethrough~ past events. Mark current/next with ← _agora_/_próximo_

:wrench: _Dev (por prioridade)_
Numbered list of dev-related Active tasks (with :hourglass_flowing_sand:)

:clipboard: _Outras_
Non-dev Active tasks as bullets

:date: _Brevemente_
Waiting On tasks as bullets

:calendar: _Amanhã_
Tomorrow's events (only if any)

Use Portuguese day names: Seg, Ter, Qua, Qui, Sex.

STEP 4: Send via Slack
source .env
curl -s -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage -H "Authorization: Bearer $SLACK_BOT_TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"channel":"C091FP35C95","text":"<formatted_message>"}'
Replace <formatted_message> with the digest. Escape quotes properly.

Keep concise. Bullet points, no prose.

jobs.description: Check Slack and WhatsApp for new task commands
jobs.prompt:
You are Ekus checking for new messages. Working directory: ~/ekus

STEP 1: Load state
source .env
Read config/message-checker-state.json for last_slack_ts and last_whatsapp_check.
If file doesn't exist, create it with: {"last_slack_ts": "0", "last_whatsapp_check": ""}

STEP 2: Check Slack DM
Fetch recent messages from Goncalo's DM channel:
curl -s "https://slack.com/api/conversations.history?channel=D0ADZB7D733&oldest=$LAST_SLACK_TS&limit=20" -H "Authorization: Bearer $SLACK_BOT_TOKEN"
Filter messages where user is U091FP30H9V (Goncalo). Ignore bot messages.

STEP 3: Check WhatsApp
Search recent messages:
wacli messages search "" --chat "120363343316234999@g.us" --limit 10
Filter for messages from +351936256982 that are newer than last_whatsapp_check.

STEP 4: Process new messages
For each new message from Goncalo:
- If it looks like a task request ("add task", "lembra-me", "tarefa", "task", "todo"):
  Create a Trello card in the a_fazer list:
  curl -s -X POST "https://api.trello.com/1/cards?key=$TRELLO_KEY&token=$TRELLO_TOKEN&idList=692ef0efcda3c6ad22f446b0&name=<task_name>"
  Reply acknowledging the task was added.
- If it looks like a scheduling request ("schedule", "agendar", "marca"):
  Create Trello card + calendar event via gcal_create_event MCP tool.
  Reply confirming.
- If it's something Ekus can do autonomously (search, check info, etc.):
  Do it and reply with the result.
- Otherwise: reply saying you noted it.

STEP 5: Reply
- Slack: reply to channel D0ADZB7D733 using curl -s -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage with Authorization Bearer $SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
- WhatsApp: wacli send text --to "120363343316234999@g.us" --message "<reply>"

STEP 6: Update state
Write updated timestamps to config/message-checker-state.json.
For Slack: use the ts of the latest message processed.
For WhatsApp: use current ISO timestamp.

If no new messages found, exit silently (no replies needed).
